scope: Render web and worker service configuration

high_probability_memory_risks.risk: single worker process runs token daemon, watchdog, and job scheduler as long-running threads
high_probability_memory_risks.impact: if any daemon leaks memory, Render worker process can restart
high_probability_memory_risks.action: add safe memory snapshot logging and isolate heavy jobs if memory climbs

high_probability_memory_risks.risk: repo contains multiple long-running loops that need bounded memory review
high_probability_memory_risks.impact: unbounded caches/lists/dataframes may accumulate over time
high_probability_memory_risks.action: audit each active runtime loop and add cleanup/TTL/bounds

high_probability_memory_risks.risk: option-chain and market-data processing can create large objects
high_probability_memory_risks.impact: sudden memory spikes during fetch/parse/model jobs
high_probability_memory_risks.action: free large temporary objects after each batch and avoid retaining full chain history in RAM

high_probability_memory_risks.risk: Render service logs and memory graphs are not available from GitHub repo alone
high_probability_memory_risks.impact: exact OOM root cause is not fully proven yet
high_probability_memory_risks.action: collect last 3-5 minutes of Render logs before restart

required_next_actions: Collect Render logs immediately before the OOM restart
Patch cloud_worker.py with safe RSS memory snapshot logs
Run static loop/cache audit for active runtime files
Move heavy dashboard/browser proof outside Render web runtime
Upgrade Render plan only after memory growth pattern is known
final_recommendation: Do not upgrade RAM first. First add memory logs, collect Render logs, and identify whether memory climbs steadily or spikes during scheduled jobs.
